title: 10 Second TNT
description: A datapack that spawns TNT on top of you every 10 seconds.
body:
# 10 Second TNT 10 Second TNT is a datapack that summons TNT on top of you every 10 seconds. It supports easy customization, you can easily adjust TNT spawning speed, and it's easy to enable and disable the timer.

Great for a fun challenge by yourself or with friends. ## Features - The TNT spawning speed can be easily adjusted in game. - `/trigger tnt_spawn_speed set [time in seconds]` - You can easily disable the actionbar timer by running this command. - `/trigger disable_tnt_actionbar set [0/1]` ## Use 1. Once you're in your world you can type `/function tnt:init` to initialize the datapack 2. Use `/trigger` to adjust the settings as seen in the *Features* section 3. Run `/function tnt:start` to start the TNT spawning. A 10 second grace period is given 4. Run `/function tnt:stop` when or if you want to stop tnt from spawning. ## Commands **`/function tnt:init`** - Initalizes all parts of the datapack, run this before trying to adjust anything with `/trigger` **`/function tnt:disable`** - Removes all scoreboards related to the datapack. Do this if you want to return a world back to normal and get rid of unneccessary scoreboards **`/function tnt:start`** - Starts TNT spawning and gives a 10 second grace period. **`/function tnt:stop`** - Disables TNT spawning. ## Installation 1. Download the datapack and place it on your desktop, or a spot that's easy to drag onto the game. 2. Create a new world and select the datapacks button. 3. Click and drag the datapack `.zip` file into the Minecraft game and click yes. 4. Hover over the datapack and click the arrow on the datapack to move it from the available to the selected datapacks. 5. Make sure to turn Allow Cheats **ON** so you can start the datapack 6. Create your world with your preferred settings of choice. ## Inspiration **This entire project has been inspired by Technoblade, rest in peace.**
